description: Perform a Functional Principal Component Analysis (FPCA) on the female cohort of the Berkeley Growth Study data from the `fda` package to quantify the dominant modes of variation in height. Smooth the height data into functional data objects using a B-spline basis system before performing the FPCA. Save the variance explained by the first four harmonics to ~/RProjects/output/fpca_variance.csv. Generate a plot of the mean growth curve and save it to ~/RProjects/output/mean_growth_curve.png. Additionally, generate a plot of the first principal component (Harmonic 1) showing the mean +/- perturbation and save it to ~/RProjects/output/pc1_variation.png. Save the complete analysis script to ~/RProjects/growth_analysis.R.
